openings.name: London System
openings.description: The d4–Bf4 system: solid structure, easy plans, hard to break.

openings.name: Caro-Kann Defence
openings.description: Black meets 1.e4 with c6: Advance, Exchange, Classical, Fantasy, Panov and sidelines.

openings.name: Wayward Queen Attack
openings.description: 2.Qh5 and Scholar’s Mate tricks — deadly if unprepared, punishable if not.

openings.name: Napoleon Attack
openings.description: 2.Qf3 aiming at f7 — another premature queen raid, refuted by development.

openings.name: Greco Defence (early …Qf6)
openings.description: Black’s 2…Qf6 mirror of the scholar’s-mate idea — meet it with tempo.

openings.name: Englund Gambit
openings.description: 1.d4 e5!? — Black’s trap-laden gambit; sound play refutes it, greed gets mated.

openings.name: Fried Liver Attack
openings.description: Italian with Ng5 hunting f7 — sharp play for both sides of the Two Knights.

openings.name: Italian Game
openings.description: Quiet development, long-term pressure: Giuoco Piano and Pianissimo.

openings.name: Ruy Lopez
openings.description: The Spanish: 3.Bb5 — closed main lines and the Exchange variation.

openings.name: Sicilian Defence
openings.description: Black’s sharpest reply to 1.e4 — Najdorf and Taimanov structures.

openings.name: French Defence
openings.description: Solid e6/d5 counterplay: Advance, Winawer and Tarrasch lines.

openings.name: Queen’s Gambit
openings.description: 1.d4 d5 2.c4 — Declined, Slav and Accepted structures.

openings.name: Vienna Game
openings.description: 2.Nc3 keeping f4 in reserve — the Vienna Gambit and quiet lines.
